title: tell everything about end semester exam...

messages.content: tell everything about end semester exam

End Semester Examination:

- Students are eligible to appear in the supplementary examination if they actually appeared at the last end-semester examination and obtained the grade 'F'.
- Absence due to medical reasons or calamities in the family will be considered for supplementary with full credit.
- Applications must be submitted in writing to the Dean (Undergraduate Studies) through the Head of the Department.
- Medical cases will be put up for consideration to the medical board.
- Full credit will be granted upon certification by the medical board.
- Only five subjects per student can be taken in the supplementary examinations and Summer Quarter combined.
- Intentions to appear for the supplementary examination must be made online through ERP.
- Counter-signed applications from teachers or HODs are no longer required.
- Grades in the subject scored in the supplementary examination will be recomputed by substituting the marks of the end-semester examination into the total marks scored.
- A maximum of one grade lower than the actual score is allowed, except for the performance grade 'P', which remains unchanged.
- Final grades awarded must be sent to the Academic section on or before the specified date in the academic calendar.
- Starting from the academic year 2016-2017, only first-year and final-year students are eligible to appear in the supplementary examination if they actually appeared and obtained a grade 'F'.
- Students experiencing poor results in mid-term exams can seek help through various resources including Tickle Academy and YouTube playlists.
- The grading scheme change from Spring 2018 to Spring 2019 had significant impacts on the grading scale, leading to higher failure rates.
- Tips for cracking the paper included being thorough with concepts, relying less on slides, solving sums from recommended textbooks, and reading additional resources like AP French book and Ajoy Ghatak's book on optics.
